concerns.text: T009-hash (zero-overlap verification) is listed as a dependency for T009-record, but T009-gen (data generation) is the actual producer of the topology IDs. T009-hash cannot execute until T009-gen completes. The current list implies T009-hash might run before T009-gen or in parallel, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule. T009-hash must strictly follow T009-gen.

T016 (Integrate GFM and Solver) lists dependencies on T009-gen, but T014 (Symbolic Solver) and T014a (Differentiable Wrapper) are listed as separate tasks without explicit ordering relative to T016. T016 consumes the output of T014/T014a. The task list must ensure T014 and T014a precede T016 to satisfy the 'Producer before Consumer' dependency.

T019 (Latent drift detection) depends on T010b (Reference stats), but T010b is in Phase 3 (US1) while T019 is in Phase 4 (US2). While cross-phase dependencies are allowed, the task list must explicitly mark T019 as dependent on the completion of T010b. Currently, the dependency is implied by phase order but not explicit in the task description, risking execution before stats are generated.

T022a (Baseline Runner) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on T009-gen (data generation). If T009-gen is not yet complete, T022a cannot run. The [P] tag is misleading here; it should only be [P] if the data generation is guaranteed complete or if the task includes a wait/dependency check. Given the strict data-flow, T022a is not parallel-safe with respect to T009-gen.

T008a and T008b (profiling and budget calculation) are listed as pending but depend on T007 (config) and potentially T009 (data generation) to simulate actual runtimes. The current ordering places them in Phase 2 (Foundational) before data generation (Phase 3). This creates a logical gap: how can one profile 'solver execution' or calculate 'total experiment time' without the actual solver logic (T014) or data (T009) being available? These tasks should be moved to Phase 4 or marked as dependent on T014/T009.
